title: Make Viral AI Art Now

modules.title: Why AI Art Goes Viral: The Psychology of Shareable Visuals
modules.sub: Decode why certain images stop the scroll every time
modules.description: Learners analyze real examples of viral AI art to identify the psychological triggers that drive shares, saves, and comments. By the end of the module, they can articulate what makes an image feel compelling versus forgettable and apply that lens to their own creative decisions.

modules.title: Caricature Styles and Trends: What's Blowing Up Right Now
modules.sub: Map the styles dominating feeds and spot emerging looks
modules.description: Learners survey the caricature and stylized portrait trends currently performing across platforms, from hyper-detailed painterly looks to bold flat-graphic styles. They identify two or three styles that match their personal taste and audience potential, setting a creative direction for the rest of the course.

modules.title: Prompt Engineering for Visual Impact: Getting the Look You Want
modules.sub: Write prompts that reliably produce striking, on-target images
modules.description: Learners practice building and refining prompts using structure, style references, lighting cues, and negative prompts to steer AI tools toward a specific visual outcome. Through rapid iteration, they develop a personal prompt template they can reuse and adapt across future projects.

modules.title: Building a Consistent Aesthetic Across Your Content
modules.sub: Turn scattered posts into a recognizable, cohesive visual brand
modules.description: Learners define the core visual variables — color palette, mood, subject framing, and style — that will unify their content as a series rather than a collection of one-offs. They produce a small set of test images to pressure-check whether the aesthetic holds together before committing to a full content run.

modules.title: Platforms, Posting Strategy, and Growing an Audience
modules.sub: Choose your platform and build a posting plan that compounds
modules.description: Learners evaluate which platform best fits their style and goals, then map out a realistic posting cadence, caption approach, and hashtag or discovery strategy tailored to that platform. They leave with a written content calendar for their first two weeks of publishing.

modules.title: Turning Followers Into Income: Commissions, Licensing, and More
modules.sub: Explore real revenue paths AI artists are using today
modules.description: Learners survey the main income models available to AI artists — including commissions, digital product sales, print-on-demand, and licensing — and assess which align with their audience size and content style. They draft a simple offer or pricing structure they could realistically launch within the month.

modules.title: Ethics, Copyright, and Giving Credit in AI Art
modules.sub: Navigate the rules and responsibilities that come with AI creativity
modules.description: Learners examine the current debates around AI art and intellectual property, including questions about training data, artist attribution, and platform policies. They make informed decisions about disclosure practices and develop a personal stance they can communicate clearly to their audience.

modules.title: Your Portfolio Drop: Launch Something Real This Week
modules.sub: Ship a real series and go live with your content strategy
modules.description: Learners finalize a cohesive series of at least three to five AI-generated images, write the accompanying captions, and publish them on their chosen platform using the strategy they built throughout the course. They conduct a brief self-review after posting to identify what to keep, adjust, or experiment with in their next content cycle.

synopsis: You've seen AI caricatures and stylized portraits flood your feed — this course shows you exactly how they're made and how to make ones people actually share. By the end, you'll have a personal art style, a posting strategy, and a clear path to turning your creations into something more than likes.
rationale: Visual AI content is one of the fastest-growing creative opportunities for young adults, but most AI literacy courses skip the hands-on, platform-aware skills needed to actually build an audience or income from it.
learning_outcome: Learners can create a cohesive series of AI-generated caricatures or stylized images and launch them on at least one platform with a repeatable content strategy.
